name: python3-pygments
title: python3-pygments
summary: syntax highlighting package written in Python 3
description:
syntax highlighting package written in Python 3
Pygments aims to be a generic syntax highlighter for general use in all kinds
of software such as forum systems, wikis or other applications that need to
prettify source code.
.
Highlights are:
* a wide range of common languages and markup formats is supported
* special attention is paid to details, increasing quality by a fair amount
* support for new languages and formats are added easily
* a number of output formats, presently HTML, LaTeX and ANSI sequences
* it is usable as a command-line tool and as a library

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use pygmentize because syntax highlighting package written in Python 3.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use pygmentize as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pygmentize        [-l <lexer> | -g] [-F <filter>[:<options>]] [-f <formatter>]        [-O <op-` as documented upstream for pygmentize.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pygmentize -S <style> -f <formatter> [-a <arg>] [-O <options>] [-P <option=value>]` as documented upstream for pygmentize.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pygmentize -L [<which> ...]` as documented upstream for pygmentize.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pygmentize -N <filename>` as documented upstream for pygmentize.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair pygmentize with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pygmentize --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install pygments. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `pygmentize        [-l <lexer> | -g] [-F <filter>[:<options>]] [-f <formatter>]        [-O <op-`. Further invocations from upstream: `pygmentize -S <style> -f <formatter> [-a <arg>] [-O <options>] [-P <option=value>]`; `pygmentize -L [<which> ...]`. syntax highlighting package written in Python 3.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: syntax highlighting package written in Python 3.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: syntax highlighting package written in Python 3.

Install with: sudo apt install pygments
